data.title: Ultra Clean Q1 revenue rises, beats analyst expectations
data.description:
Overview

  • Semiconductor subsystems supplier’s Q1 revenue rose sequentially and beat analysts’ expectations

  • Q1 non-GAAP net income positive, but company posted wider GAAP net loss

  • Company cites strong customer demand and focus on operational efficiencies

Outlook

  • Company sees Q2 2026 GAAP diluted EPS between $0.20 and $0.36

Result Drivers

  • TECHNOLOGY ROADMAPS - Customers accelerating technology roadmaps contributed to growth, per CEO James Xiao

data.title: SDA: 2025 revenue rose 11% to $489.3M, net loss narrowed, and EV/tech partnerships expanded
data.description:
Revenue grew 11% to $489.3M in 2025, led by EV insurance and technology services, while net loss narrowed to $2.4M. The company expanded partnerships with major EV OEMs, invested in AI/cloud, and completed a $50M equity offering and $30M share buyback.

Original document: SunCar Technology Group Inc. [SDA] SEC 20-F Annual Report — May. 5 2026

data.title: F5 beats Q2 revenue estimates, raises 2026 revenue growth forecast
data.description:
Overview

  • US application security platform's fiscal Q2 revenue grew 11%, beating analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EPS for fiscal Q2 beat consensus

  • Company raised full-year revenue and adjusted EPS guidance

Outlook

  • F5 raises FY2026 revenue growth outlook to 7%-8% from 5%-6% previously

Result Drivers

  • PRODUCT REVENUE - Co said 22% product revenue growth drove overall Q2 revenue gains

  • STRUCTURAL DEMAND DRIVERS - Co attributed rising demand to hybrid multicloud adoption, expanding cybersecurity threats, and inflection in AI inference

data.title: Northern Oil and Gas Q1 adjusted EPS beats estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • US oil and gas producer's Q1 adjusted EPS beat analyst expectations

  • Adjusted EBITDA fell 21% yr/yr due to lower realized prices and commodity mix

  • Company posted large net loss on non-cash derivative loss and impairment charges

Outlook

  • Company expects acceleration of wells turned in line through remainder of 2026

  • Company sees improved field-level price realizations, especially in the Williston Basin

Result Drivers

  • PRODUCTION GROWTH - Co said total production rose 10% yr/yr and natural gas output hit a record, up 33% from Q1 2025

  • LOWER REALIZED PRICES - Adjusted EBITDA fell 21% yr/yr, reflecting a 19% decrease in realized price on a Boe basis including settled commodity derivatives and the impact of a changing commodity mix

  • PERMIAN GAS CONSTRAINTS - Natural gas realizations continued to be pressured due to lack of takeaway capacity in the Permian Basin

data.title: T-Mobile Stock Rises on Earnings Beat — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Angela Palumbo

T-Mobile US stock was up in late trading on Tuesday after the telecommunications company reported better-than-expected financial results.

The wireless carrier posted first-quarter adjusted earnings of $2.27 a share on revenue of $23.1 billion. Analysts surveyed by FactSet were expecting earnings of $2.01 a share on revenue of $23 billion.

Shares were up 1.7% in after-hours trading following the results.

This is breaking news. Please come back for more updates and read below for a preview of the earnings.

T-Mobile US is the next major U.S. telecommunications provider to report earnings, and Wall Street will be watching for more than just a financial beat following reports of a possible mega-merger.

T-Mobile is scheduled to report first-quarter financial results after the stock market closes on Tuesday. Analysts surveyed by FactSet expect the company to post adjusted earnings of $2.01 a share on revenue of $22.98 billion.

Wall Street is also expecting T-Mobile to add 393,100 net phone postpaid subscribers over the period.

Shareholders will be listening closely for any information the company shares regarding merger speculation. Specifically, Bloomberg reported on April 21 that German telecom provider Deutsche Telekom AG is weighing a merger with T-Mobile. The report said that discussions were preliminary and that any deal would require the political support of both the German and U.S. governments.

T-Mobile told Barron's in an email following the report that "as per our usual practice, T-Mobile and DT do not comment on speculation regarding our corporate activity, nor are there specifics to share."

T-Mobile stock dropped 1.5% on April 21.

Shares have fallen 8.8% this year. Some investors are worried that a price war between T-Mobile and its competitors AT&T and Verizon Communications could weigh on the company's bottom line. That risk could increase if T-Mobile is forced to roll out aggressive promotions following a potential release of a foldable iPhone, which is expected to carry a high price tag.

AT&T and Verizon have already posted their first-quarter financial results. Both companies reported better-than-expected earnings and postpaid net phone additions.

Write to Angela Palumbo at angela.palumbo@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Seagate forecasts upbeat quarter as AI boom powers strong data-storage demand
data.description:
Seagate Technology forecast fourth-quarter revenue and profit above Wall Street expectations on Tuesday, betting on strong demand for its data-storage hardware as enterprises ramp up adoption of artificial intelligence.

Shares of the company were up around 10% in extended trading.

Enterprises rushing to incorporate AI into their businesses to speed up processes and cut costs have also invested in storage to handle the vast amount of data required to create and run the latest models.

Seagate's shares have doubled so far this year after more than tripling in 2025, as the AI-led boom in demand and a surge in memory chip prices kindled investor enthusiasm in the storage market.

Memory chip prices rose between 80% to 90% sequentially in the first quarter of this year, according to Counterpoint Research data from February.

Seagate is battling intense competition from companies such as Western Digital as they rush to ramp up production and build capacity to secure new contracts with cloud providers.

The company forecast fourth-quarter revenue of $3.45 billion, plus or minus $100 million, compared with estimates of $3.16 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.

The company expects quarterly adjusted earnings per share of $5, plus or minus 20 cents, while analysts expect $3.97.

Seagate reported third-quarter revenue of $3.11 billion, beating estimates of $2.96 billion. It reported earnings per share of $3.27, compared with $1.57 per share a year earlier.

data.title: T-Mobile 1Q Revenue Rises on Postpaid Account Growth
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

T-Mobile US logged higher revenue for the first quarter as it continued to grow its base of postpaid accounts.

The cell carrier said Tuesday it had 217,000 postpaid net account additions in the first quarter, including in phone and broadband, up 6% year over year. The company's postpaid phone service is its most lucrative phone offering, where service is paid for each month on a contract. Analysts polled by FactSet had forecast 192,800 postpaid net account additions in the quarter.

Average monthly postpaid service revenue earned per account also rose, up 3.9% year-over-year to $151.93.

Chief Executive Srini Gopalan said those metrics show the company is both attracting new customers and deepening its engagement with its existing base.

"We're more confident than ever in our strategy and the opportunities ahead of us," Gopalan said. "The best truly lies ahead of us."

The growth in postpaid accounts helped T-Mobile's total sales climb almost 11% in the quarter and top Wall Street's expectations.

First-quarter revenue was $23.11 billion, compared with analyst estimates of $22.98 billion.

First-quarter profit came in at $2.5 billion, or $2.27 a share, compared with $2.95 billion, or $2.58 a share, a year earlier. The recent quarter's figure includes merger-related costs related to T-Mobile's acquisition of USCellular, including accelerated depreciation, net of tax, of $476 million. Analysts polled by FactSet had expected earnings of $2.01 a share.

For the full year, T-Mobile now expects to add 950,000 to 1.05 million postpaid net accounts, up from its prior guidance for 900,000 to 1 million additions.

The Bellevue, Wash., company also bumped up the midpoint of its forecast for core adjusted earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization to a range of $37.1 billion to $37.5 billion, compared with prior guidance of $37 billion to $37.5 billion.

The results and guidance come after The Wall Street Journal reported last week that T-Mobile and its majority owner, Germany's Deutsche Telekom, are discussing combining to create a more streamlined telecom behemoth. Deutsche Telekom has grown its T-Mobile stake over the years and Gopalan, a former Deutsche Telekom executive, took over as T-Mobile CEO at the end of last year.

T-Mobile said earlier Tuesday it agreed to form two strategic fiber joint ventures, with planned investments totaling $2.7 billion. One 50/50 partnership with private-equity firm Oak Hill Capital will acquire and combine GoNetspeed and Greenlight Networks, while the other is with Wren House to acquire i3 Broadband. The partnerships will expand T-Mobile's fiber footprint, the company said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Edison International Q1 core EPS beats profit estimates
data.description:
Overview

  • U.S. electric utility's Q1 core EPS rose yr/yr, beating analyst expectations

  • Company affirmed 2026 core EPS guidance and 5-7% growth outlook through 2030

  • Core EPS increase driven by adoption of 2025 GRC final decision, partly offset by lower cost recoveries

Outlook

  • Edison International affirms 2026 core EPS guidance of $5.90-$6.20

  • Company maintains expectation of 5-7% core EPS growth from 2025-2030

Result Drivers

  • REGULATORY DECISION - Southern California Edison’s Q1 core EPS rose mainly due to adoption of the 2025 GRC final decision

  • LOWER PREFERRED DIVIDENDS - Parent and Other's core loss per share narrowed mainly due to lower preferred stock dividends

data.title: Apple Inc expected to post earnings of $1.95 a share - Earnings Preview
data.description:
• Apple Inc is expected to show a rise in quarterly revenue when it reports results on April 30 for the period ending March 31 2026

  • The Cupertino California-based company is expected to report a 15.0% increase in revenue to $109.66 billion from $95.36 billion a year ago, according to the mean estimate from 27 analysts, based on LSEG data.The company's guidance on January 29 2026, for the period ended March 31, was for revenue between $107.76 billion and $110.62 billion. (Revenue Majority is on Total Revenue basis)

  • ​LSEG's mean analyst estimate for Apple Inc is for earnings of $1.95 per share.

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 33 "strong buy" or "buy," 13 "hold" and 2 "sell" or "strong sell."

  • The mean earnings estimate of analysts was unchanged in the last three months. ​

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Apple Inc is $300.00, about 12.1% above its last closing price of $267.61

The company's guidance on January 29 2026 for the period ended March 31 was for gross profit margin between USD48% and USD49%.

data.title: Bloom Energy Raises Outlook. The Stock Is Rising. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By Liz Moyer

Bloom Energy shares jumped in after hours trading after it reported record first quarter results that beat expectations.

The company reported first quarter adjusted earnings of 44 cents a share and revenue of $751 million. Wall Street expected adjusted earnings of 12 cents a share and revenue of $539 million

The stock was up 8% in late trading.

This is a breaking news story. Check back here for details.

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Visa Q2 adjusted EPS rises beats analyst estimates driven by increase in payments volume
data.description:
Overview

  • US digital payments giant's fiscal Q2 net revenue rose 17% yr/yr

  • Adjusted EPS for fiscal Q2 beat analyst expectations, rising 20% yr/yr

  • Company authorized new $20 bln multi-year share repurchase program

Outlook

  • Visa did not provide specific financial guidance for the current or upcoming quarters

Result Drivers

  • PAYMENTS VOLUME GROWTH - Visa said net revenue growth was driven by a 9% yr/yr increase in payments volume

  • CROSS-BORDER VOLUME - Cross-border volume excluding intra-Europe rose 11% yr/yr, supporting international transaction revenue

  • PROCESSED TRANSACTIONS - Processed transactions increased 9% yr/yr to 66.1 bln
